data.body: Detailed implementation plan committed at docs/bash-migration.md (73be64b, task prim-myth-grain): tier B-E scope tables, per-tier kill-switches (REBAR_LEAF_WRITES/REBAR_COMPUTE/REBAR_WRITE_CORE) with the REBAR_PUSH parsing idiom, the 7-step per-command protocol generalized from REBAR_NATIVE_READS (7d53bc5b->a93885e), the Tier B event-append seam preserving I5, the Tier D dual-window lock-interop rule (fcntl+mkdir both-legs acquisition), and the concurrency validation matrix (mixed-impl same-clone storms, two-clone cross-core convergence, mixed-version fleets). docs/architecture.md module-size section now anchors AC #1 to it. Planning surfaced bug stiff-mop-lane (bash mkdir-fallback writes don't contend with ticket_txn.py fcntl.flock on flock(1)-less platforms) — child of this story, fix due no later than Tier D.
